name: Telegram Mini App runtime hardening and bundle reduction
description: Harden the TMA startup/runtime path, auth-storage fallback behavior, safe-area reactivity, and route loading so the Mini App launches reliably and loads less code on cold start.

planned: Catch Telegram SDK launch failures before React render and route them into a renderable fatal state.
Make TMA safe-area CSS vars reactive after viewport/fullscreen changes instead of sync-once behavior.
Trip auth storage permanently into memory-only mode after secure read/delete failures and simplify bootstrap dead branches.
Lazy-load route modules to reduce the current eager TMA startup bundle.
Collapse duplicated lightweight helpers only after runtime/auth correctness fixes land.
artifacts: Added `initTelegramSafely()` and threaded startup failure handling through `apps/tma/src/main.tsx` and `apps/tma/src/app/app.tsx` so missing Telegram launch params now render the existing fatal launch UI instead of crashing before React mounts.
Reworked `apps/tma/src/lib/telegram/theme.ts` to bind `miniApp`, `themeParams`, and `viewport` CSS vars once, keep safe-area vars reactive via `viewport.bindCssVars()`, and clean them up on teardown.
Hardened `apps/tma/src/lib/storage/adapter.ts` so secure read/delete failures now trip the adapter into explicit memory-only mode instead of silently retrying broken SecureStorage calls forever.

Removed currently unreachable bootstrap branches in `apps/tma/src/features/auth/bootstrap.tsx`, `apps/tma/src/features/auth/bootstrap-deps.ts`, and `apps/tma/src/features/auth/store.ts` without changing the existing authenticated/fatal outcomes.

Lazy-loaded the TMA route modules in `apps/tma/src/app/router/app-router.tsx`, removed the duplicate explicit `/home` route, and preserved the same page components behind `Suspense` + `LoadingFallback`.

Tightened shared shell back-navigation in `apps/tma/src/components/shared/tma-page-shell.tsx` so SPA back now depends only on React Router's own `history.state.idx` and never on browser-history length outside the Mini App stack.

Added a focused shell navigation regression test covering SPA back and cold-detail fallback, preserved create-household partial-failure feedback across navigation, cached TMA currency formatters, removed the dead `AuthBootstrap.loadingFallback` prop, and reused shared `closeMiniApp` in both fatal screens.

Extracted `apps/tma/src/features/expenses/presentation.ts` so `getSourceLabel()` and `buildHouseholdNameMap()` are shared across `ExpensesPage`, `ExpenseDetailPage`, and `ExpenseEditPage` instead of being repeated in each route.

Moved the TMA-only `getCurrentPeriod()` helper into `apps/tma/src/lib/period.ts`, moved `MAX_AVATAR_SIZE_BYTES` into `apps/tma/src/lib/media/constants.ts`, and updated the TMA household/home call sites to use those dedicated modules instead of burying small shared values in presentation files.
verification: Focused TMA verification passed on 2026-06-04: `pnpm --filter tma exec vitest run src/test/home-presentation.test.ts src/test/storage-adapter.test.ts src/test/theme.test.ts src/test/telegram-init.test.ts src/test/auth-bootstrap.test.ts src/test/safe-area.test.ts` passed with 24 tests across 6 files.

Focused TMA build verification passed on 2026-06-04: `pnpm --filter tma build` succeeded after lazy routes and formatter caching, emitting split route chunks and reducing the eager entry bundle to `436.00 kB` minified / `139.30 kB` gzip.
Repo canonical verification passed on 2026-06-04: `./init.sh lint` returned `OK`, `./init.sh build` returned `OK`, and final `./init.sh` completed with `Done!`.
Repo hygiene checks passed on 2026-06-04: `git diff --check` returned clean.

Focused shell/navigation verification passed on 2026-06-04: `pnpm --filter tma exec vitest run src/test/tma-page-shell-navigation.test.tsx src/test/scroll-restoration.test.tsx src/test/theme.test.ts src/test/telegram-init.test.ts src/test/storage-adapter.test.ts src/test/auth-bootstrap.test.ts src/test/home-presentation.test.ts` passed with 27 tests across 7 files.

Repo-wide follow-up verification passed on 2026-06-04 after the continued refactor: `./init.sh typecheck`, `./init.sh lint`, `./init.sh test`, `./init.sh build`, and final `./init.sh` all completed successfully (`OK` / `Done!`).

Final `gitnexus_detect_changes(scope: 'all', repo: 'household-finance-system')` reported `critical` risk with 5 changed files / 12 changed symbols / 49 affected processes for the isolated shell/auth cleanup follow-up, concentrated in `TmaPageShell`, `AuthBootstrap`, and the router.

Focused helper-extraction verification passed on 2026-06-04: `pnpm --filter tma exec vitest run src/test/expense-presentation.test.ts src/test/tma-page-shell-navigation.test.tsx src/test/home-presentation.test.ts` passed with 9 tests across 3 files, `pnpm --filter tma typecheck` passed, `./init.sh lint` returned `OK`, `./init.sh test` returned `OK`, and final `./init.sh` again completed with `Done!`.

Final GitNexus check for the helper-extraction slice reported `high` risk with 3 changed files / 12 changed symbols / 14 affected processes, limited to the expected expense routes (`ExpensesPage`, `ExpenseDetailPage`, `ExpenseEditPage`).

Focused period/media-helper verification passed on 2026-06-04: `pnpm --filter tma exec vitest run src/test/period.test.ts src/test/home-presentation.test.ts src/test/household-presentation.test.ts src/test/expense-presentation.test.ts` passed with 10 tests across 4 files, `pnpm --filter tma typecheck` passed, `./init.sh lint` returned `OK`, `./init.sh test` returned `OK`, and final `./init.sh` completed with `Done!`.

Final GitNexus check for the period/media-helper slice reported `low` risk with 6 changed files and no changed symbols/affected processes surfaced, matching a move-only helper extraction that stayed inside the TMA surface.

notes: This feature is a hardening and architecture pass over the active TMA worktree. It intentionally prioritizes runtime correctness and cold-start behavior over new product scope.
